## Description

And here how Google answers it:

```
To publish a package to the NPM registry, you must set up an NPM Sign Up account, initialize your project locally with a package.json file, and run the  command. [[1](https://www.freecodecamp.org/news/how-to-publish-an-npm-package/#:~:text=Method%202%20%2D%20You%20Run%20npm%20install%20Without%20Existing%20Dependencies.&text=Then%20npm%20will%20run:%20*%20Resolve%20the%20package%20version:%20It%20fetches%20the%20latest%20v)]  
Here is the complete, step-by-step process to prepare and publish your code. 
1. Set Up Your NPM Account 

• Create an account: Sign up for free on the official  NPM Sign Up  page. 
• Authenticate your terminal: Open your terminal and run the npm login Docs command: 
• Verify credentials: Follow the terminal prompts to input your username, password, and Email OTP. [[1](https://www.freecodecamp.org/news/how-to-publish-an-npm-package/#:~:text=Method%202%20%2D%20You%20Run%20npm%20install%20Without%20Existing%20Dependencies.&text=Then%20npm%20will%20run:%20*%20Resolve%20the%20package%20version:%20It%20fetches%20the%20latest%20v), [3](https://docs.npmjs.com/cli/v6/commands/npm-publish/#:~:text=Description%20*%20:%20A%20folder%20containing%20a,gzipped%20tar%20archive%20containing%20a%20single%20folder)]  

2. Initialize Your Project 

• Create a folder: Build a new directory for your library and navigate into it: 
• Generate package.json: Initialize the configuration file by running  npm init Docs : 
• Fill out configuration: Complete the terminal prompts with these core details: 

	• name: A completely unique name across the registry. Check availability via NPM Search. 
	• version: Start at . 
	• main: The entry point file, usually . [[1](https://www.freecodecamp.org/news/how-to-publish-an-npm-package/#:~:text=Method%202%20%2D%20You%20Run%20npm%20install%20Without%20Existing%20Dependencies.&text=Then%20npm%20will%20run:%20*%20Resolve%20the%20package%20version:%20It%20fetches%20the%20latest%20v), [7](https://dev.to/goodness-chukwudi/a-step-by-step-guide-on-how-to-create-and-publish-and-npm-package-4ae5)]  

3. Add Code and Documentation 

• Write your entry file: Create your  file and write the code you wish to export: 
• Write a README.md: Provide standard documentation detailing how users can install and use your module. 
• Create an .npmignore file: List files like , , or test suites to keep your bundle clean: [[1](https://www.freecodecamp.org/news/how-to-publish-an-npm-package/#:~:text=Method%202%20%2D%20You%20Run%20npm%20install%20Without%20Existing%20Dependencies.&text=Then%20npm%20will%20run:%20*%20Resolve%20the%20package%20version:%20It%20fetches%20the%20latest%20v), [9](https://dev.to/leopold/build-and-publish-your-npm-package-48mb)]  

4. Publish Your Package 
Run the publishing sequence depending on your package configuration type: [[10](https://docs.npmjs.com/creating-and-publishing-scoped-public-packages/#:~:text=Creating%20and%20publishing%20scoped%20public%20packages.&text=To%20share%20your%20code%20publicly%20in%20a,you%20can%20publish%20public%20user%2Dscoped%20or%20organ), [11](https://docs.npmjs.com/creating-and-publishing-unscoped-public-packages/#:~:text=Creating%20an%20unscoped%20public%20package%20*%20On,my%2Dtest%2Dpackage.%20*%20Navigate%20to%20the%20root%20directo)]  
Option A: Unscoped Public Package (Standard Name) If your package has a standard name like , deploy it directly: [[1](https://www.freecodecamp.org/news/how-to-publish-an-npm-package/#:~:text=Method%202%20%2D%20You%20Run%20npm%20install%20Without%20Existing%20Dependencies.&text=Then%20npm%20will%20run:%20*%20Resolve%20the%20package%20version:%20It%20fetches%20the%20latest%20v), [11](https://docs.npmjs.com/creating-and-publishing-unscoped-public-packages/#:~:text=Creating%20an%20unscoped%20public%20package%20*%20On,my%2Dtest%2Dpackage.%20*%20Navigate%20to%20the%20root%20directo)]  
Option B: Scoped Public Package (Recommended) If your package name includes your username or organization namespace (e.g., ) to guarantee uniqueness, you must explicitly flag public access: [[1](https://www.freecodecamp.org/news/how-to-publish-an-npm-package/#:~:text=Method%202%20%2D%20You%20Run%20npm%20install%20Without%20Existing%20Dependencies.&text=Then%20npm%20will%20run:%20*%20Resolve%20the%20package%20version:%20It%20fetches%20the%20latest%20v), [10](https://docs.npmjs.com/creating-and-publishing-scoped-public-packages/#:~:text=Creating%20and%20publishing%20scoped%20public%20packages.&text=To%20share%20your%20code%20publicly%20in%20a,you%20can%20publish%20public%20user%2Dscoped%20or%20organ)]  
5. Managing Future Updates 
Once a specific version number is pushed, you cannot overwrite or reuse it. To publish updates later, automate version bumps using  npm version Docs  before re-publishing: 

• Patch updates (bug fixes): Updates  → : 
• Minor updates (new features): Updates  → : 
• Major updates (breaking changes): Updates  → : [[12](https://chanmeng666.medium.com/a-complete-guide-to-publishing-your-first-npm-package-466fbf919ee9#:~:text=*%20Preparing%20Your%20Project.%20First%2C%20ensure%20your,package.json%20file%20is%20crucial:%20%7B%20%22name%22:%20%22@yo), [14](https://www.mend.io/blog/updating-npm-packages/#:~:text=Updating%20NPM%20Packages%20%E2%80%93%20The%20Definitive%20Guide,patch%20release)%20A%20high%20chance%20that%20ther), [15](https://tahoeninja.blog/posts/display-the-solution-version-in-your-web-part/#:~:text=When%20to%20change%20major%2C%20minor%2C%20and%20patch,New%20solution%20Patch%20release%20Versioning%20rule%20Start), [16](https://medium.com/@mubashir_ejaz/getting-started-with-url-parse-modules-and-npm-in-node-js-5229de487ea2), [17](https://medium.com/@rutvikumbhani2410/versioning-a-node-js-application-using-semantic-versioning-semver-4110966a72ea#:~:text=npm%20version%20minor%20:%20Increments%20the%20MINOR,from%201.0.%200%20to%201.1.%200%20).)]  

To streamline production work, you can also connect your repository to GitHub Actions Documentation to automate deployments instantly when tagging your releases. [[18](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JKQJShP2ngI#:~:text=The%20process%20involves%20creating%20a%20GitHub%20repository,%2Dy%60%20is%20run%20to%20create%20the%20%60package.js), [19](https://dev.to/mir_mursalin_ankur/publishing-your-first-npm-package-a-real-world-guide-that-actually-helps-4l4#:~:text=Step%207:%20Choose%20Your%20Publishing%20Strategy.%20You,start%2C%20publish%20from%20command%20line.%20*%20Au)]  
Would you like to learn how to configure TypeScript compilation for your package, or do you want to set up an automated GitHub Actions workflow to handle publishing? 

AI can make mistakes, so double-check responses

[1] [https://www.freecodecamp.org/news/how-to-publish-an-npm-package/](https://www.freecodecamp.org/news/how-to-publish-an-npm-package/#:~:text=Method%202%20%2D%20You%20Run%20npm%20install%20Without%20Existing%20Dependencies.&text=Then%20npm%20will%20run:%20*%20Resolve%20the%20package%20version:%20It%20fetches%20the%20latest%20v)
[2] [https://www.nitorinfotech.com/blog/building-and-distributing-npm-packages-a-developers-guide/](https://www.nitorinfotech.com/blog/building-and-distributing-npm-packages-a-developers-guide/#:~:text=To%20publish%20npm%20packages%2C%20you%20need%20to,npm%20user%20account%2C%20and%20then%20set%20up)
[3] [https://docs.npmjs.com/cli/v6/commands/npm-publish/](https://docs.npmjs.com/cli/v6/commands/npm-publish/#:~:text=Description%20*%20:%20A%20folder%20containing%20a,gzipped%20tar%20archive%20containing%20a%20single%20folder)
[4] [https://www.udemy.com/course/npm-mastery-nodejs-package-management-publishing/](https://www.udemy.com/course/npm-mastery-nodejs-package-management-publishing/#:~:text=Create%20an%20npm%20package%20from%20scratch%20and,and%20publishing%2C%20with%20simple%20string%20utility%20functions.)
[5] [https://medium.com/@onlyapps/how-to-create-and-publish-your-own-swift-package-manager-spm-library-6704063bcc8c](https://medium.com/@onlyapps/how-to-create-and-publish-your-own-swift-package-manager-spm-library-6704063bcc8c#:~:text=Creating%20a%20Package%201%20Create%20a%20new,init%20%2D%2Dtype%20library%20.%20This%20will%20cre)
[6] [https://dev.to/danywalls/easily-create-your-own-private-npm-registry-using-verdaccio-41ef](https://dev.to/danywalls/easily-create-your-own-private-npm-registry-using-verdaccio-41ef#:~:text=After%20create%20your%20library%20and%20build%20it%2C,the%20command%20$%20npm%20publish%20%2D%2Dregistry%20http://localhost)
[7] https://dev.to/goodness-chukwudi/a-step-by-step-guide-on-how-to-create-and-publish-and-npm-package-4ae5
[8] [https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/137792](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/137792#:~:text=Write%20your%20code%20and%20add%20documentation:%20Ensure,Test%20your%20package:%20Make%20sure%20it%20wor)
[9] https://dev.to/leopold/build-and-publish-your-npm-package-48mb
[10] [https://docs.npmjs.com/creating-and-publishing-scoped-public-packages/](https://docs.npmjs.com/creating-and-publishing-scoped-public-packages/#:~:text=Creating%20and%20publishing%20scoped%20public%20packages.&text=To%20share%20your%20code%20publicly%20in%20a,you%20can%20publish%20public%20user%2Dscoped%20or%20organ)
[11] [https://docs.npmjs.com/creating-and-publishing-unscoped-public-packages/](https://docs.npmjs.com/creating-and-publishing-unscoped-public-packages/#:~:text=Creating%20an%20unscoped%20public%20package%20*%20On,my%2Dtest%2Dpackage.%20*%20Navigate%20to%20the%20root%20directo)
[12] [https://chanmeng666.medium.com/a-complete-guide-to-publishing-your-first-npm-package-466fbf919ee9](https://chanmeng666.medium.com/a-complete-guide-to-publishing-your-first-npm-package-466fbf919ee9#:~:text=*%20Preparing%20Your%20Project.%20First%2C%20ensure%20your,package.json%20file%20is%20crucial:%20%7B%20%22name%22:%20%22@yo)
[13] https://docs.npmjs.com/cli/v8/commands/npm-publish
[14] [https://www.mend.io/blog/updating-npm-packages/](https://www.mend.io/blog/updating-npm-packages/#:~:text=Updating%20NPM%20Packages%20%E2%80%93%20The%20Definitive%20Guide,patch%20release)%20A%20high%20chance%20that%20ther)
[15] [https://tahoeninja.blog/posts/display-the-solution-version-in-your-web-part/](https://tahoeninja.blog/posts/display-the-solution-version-in-your-web-part/#:~:text=When%20to%20change%20major%2C%20minor%2C%20and%20patch,New%20solution%20Patch%20release%20Versioning%20rule%20Start)
[16] https://medium.com/@mubashir_ejaz/getting-started-with-url-parse-modules-and-npm-in-node-js-5229de487ea2
[17] [https://medium.com/@rutvikumbhani2410/versioning-a-node-js-application-using-semantic-versioning-semver-4110966a72ea](https://medium.com/@rutvikumbhani2410/versioning-a-node-js-application-using-semantic-versioning-semver-4110966a72ea#:~:text=npm%20version%20minor%20:%20Increments%20the%20MINOR,from%201.0.%200%20to%201.1.%200%20).)
[18] [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JKQJShP2ngI](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JKQJShP2ngI#:~:text=The%20process%20involves%20creating%20a%20GitHub%20repository,%2Dy%60%20is%20run%20to%20create%20the%20%60package.js)
[19] [https://dev.to/mir_mursalin_ankur/publishing-your-first-npm-package-a-real-world-guide-that-actually-helps-4l4](https://dev.to/mir_mursalin_ankur/publishing-your-first-npm-package-a-real-world-guide-that-actually-helps-4l4#:~:text=Step%207:%20Choose%20Your%20Publishing%20Strategy.%20You,start%2C%20publish%20from%20command%20line.%20*%20Au)
```

Sometimes simple search is not enough, we should do search on the topic, collect all the data, and answer on `how to` questions with a guide constructed from data we collected using logic reasoning.

We also need to support [wikihow](https://www.wikihow.com/), stackoverflow and other similar resources (when available and accessible).

For some categories of questions we can integrate also with https://www.wikibooks.org, https://www.wikiversity.org, https://www.wikivoyage.org

We also may crawl search results for more specific data, that can help answer the question (again if available).

And also we can try to access GitHub's readme's, software docs websites and so on.

So all the sources should be used to collect useful information on the topic. So each `how` question can be fully answered preferably using dynamic construction of an answer.

We also need to make sure we cache for at least 7 days status of accessibility of each of the services (in each environment in our associative memory of AI system).

Also all test cases for quality assurance should have their tests pre cached from actual services. So some answers can be done fast or instantly after we verify that search results are not changed relative to their pre-cached version.

To iterate fast we should execute most tests based on pre-cached data.

Double check that we have contributing guide, that will help me not repeat myself all the time, as most of our previous issues, already have my recomendations, that I repeat to many times.

So people and AI agents will be able to use best recommendations, when contributing to our project.

We need to download all logs and data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), in which we will reconstruct timeline/sequence of events, list of each and all requirements from the issue, find root causes of the each problem, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions).

If there is not enough data to find actual root cause, add debug output and verbose mode if not present, that will allow us to find root cause on next iteration.

If issue related to any other repository/project, where we can report issues on GitHub, please do so. Each issue must contain reproducible examples, workarounds and suggestions for fix the issue in code. Also double check to fully apply requirements to entire codebase, so if we have issue in multiple places, it should be fixed in all them.

Please plan and execute everything in this single pull request, you have unlimited time and context, as context auto-compacts and you can continue indefinitely, until it is each and every requirement fully addressed, and everything is totally done.
title: Unknown prompt: Can you give me specific instructions?
